heading: Uniform of armed forces and Public Health Service
label: 702 Uniform of armed forces and Public Health Service
text: Whoever, in any place within the jurisdiction of the United States or in the Canal Zone, without authority, wears the uniform or a distinctive part thereof or anything similar to a distinctive part of the uniform of any of the armed forces of the United States, Public Health Service or any auxiliary of such, shall be fined under this title or imprisoned not more than six months, or both.
notes: 1948ActBased on , U.S.C., 1940 ed., Army and Air Force, and , U.S.C., 1940 ed., The Public Health and Welfare (, (2d paragraph); , ).section 1393 of title 10section 228 of title 42June 3, 1916, ch. 134, § 12539 Stat. 216July 1, 1944, ch. 373, § 51058 Stat. 711“Auxiliary of such” was inserted to extend protection to the uniforms of any auxiliary corps that may be established.Fine of “$250” was substituted for “$300” as being more consonant with the penalties provided for similar offenses in this chapter.Minor changes of phraseology also were made.

1949ActThis section [section 15] inserts “armed forces” in the catch line and text of , U.S.C., and thereby includes the Air Force which was formerly part of the Army. (See note to sec. 5 [of 1949 Act, set out in Legislative History note under ]). Also, it incorporates in such section the provisions of act of (ch. 188, ), which relates to this section as well as to , U.S.C. (one of the sources of such sec. 701), as it existed at the time of the enactment of the revision of title 18 and which was not incorporated in title 18 when the revision was enacted. In this connection specific reference to the Canal Zone, Guam, American Samoa, and the Virgin Islands, as contained in such act of , were omitted as covered by the phrase, “in any place within the jurisdiction of the United States,” as used in this amendment of such , U.S.C.section 702 of title 18section 244 of title 1862 Stat. 172section 1393 of title 10section 702 of title 18April 15, 1948April 15, 1948
References in TextFor definition of Canal Zone, referred to in text, see , Foreign Relations and Intercourse.section 3602(b) of Title 22
